name: Bene Wafers
recipeInstructions:
1. Place white and black sesame seeds on a sheet tray. Toast in the oven for about 10 minutes (or until lightly golden) or up to 35-40 minutes at 350°F, stirring occasionally.
2. In a bowl, combine flour, celery salt, cayenne pepper, chili powder, and butter. Mix with fingers until it resembles a coarse meal.
3. Add the toasted sesame seeds, water, and buttermilk to the mixture. Stir until the dough is smooth.
4. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to about 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick. Cut into 3/4 to 1-inch rounds using a sharp knife or round cutter.
5. Place the rounds on an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them evenly.
6. Bake at 350°F for 10-12 minutes, until lightly golden and crisp. Let cool completely before serving.
7. Serve as croutons with soup, finger food, or with a dip.
Yield: Approximately 3 dozen wafers
